content.claim: The Ministry of Education has used discretionary approval power to deny a request by a Reformed Church congregation to introduce an 8-grade gimnázium track at a school in Tiszavasvári, citing concerns that the change would reinforce local school segregation and create irreversible segregated-school situations.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Single administrative decision; does not establish whether this reflects a systematic shift in approval policy or an isolated case
Letter does not quantify predicted segregation effect or provide counterfactual scenario
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
